title: JSSDF Fanclub
wikitext:
[]: JSSDF Fanclub Post by cannonski » Sun Apr 27, 2008 8:54 pm For those of us who pity or admire the JSSDF and their associated blackops members who deal with the dirty jobs as unremembered by most fans, we salute you! All images grabbed from EoE. Evil Spec Ops commander: Heavy Bomber Launching Missile: (as seen in episode I also) VTOL Attack!: Tank Attack!: MLRS Attack!(Ok so it's getting boring now): If there are any other fellow JSSDF fans around here, then feel free to post your pics! lol

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 27, 2008 8:55 pm You have a really crappy copy of this film, don't you?

[]: Post by cannonski » Sun Apr 27, 2008 9:00 pm Perhaps. I am just glad to be able to watch it without worrying if it is in HD, etc. EDIT: I am using an ancient PIII just now due to certain problems with my primary puter.

[]: Post by BobBQ » Sun Apr 27, 2008 10:22 pm The latter-day Einsatzgruppen seem like a bit of an odd choice for idolization. Somebody post the cat pic, fast.

[]: Post by cannonski » Sun Apr 27, 2008 11:43 pm Well if the thread offends then I will gladly remove it. I merely posted it in the same way as many americans and other nationalities would post pics of battleships, etc. I'd also remind you that the JSSDF lost significant forces in episode one. Don't get me wrong, I wouldn't sympathise with the behaviour of them as witnessed in EoE, but I do believe they would deserve a little sympathy for having a tank battalion, etc facing off versus a giant green angel called Sachiel. Anyway sorry to drag politics in here.

[]: Post by The Imperialist » Wed Apr 30, 2008 5:14 am The potential future of the JSDF: I salute it.

[]: Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:23 am BobBQ wrote: The latter-day Einsatzgruppen seem like a bit of an odd choice for idolization. Somebody post the cat pic, fast.

[]: Post by BobBQ » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:44 am V wrote: ...why do you persistently state that the JSSDF if based on the military arm of the Nazi SS? serious question.

[]: Post by Xard » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:59 am So japs modeled structure of their defense forces after Germany? Cool, I didn't know that

[]: Post by The Imperialist » Wed Apr 30, 2008 9:20 am Actually, it was the Imperial Japanese Army, who initially had French training, but after the Franco-Prussian War, based their training along German lines, but the JSDF (well, the predecessors) were given AMERICAN training, not German.

[]: Post by Tabasco » Wed Apr 30, 2008 11:44 am Xard wrote: So japs modeled structure of their defense forces after Germany? Cool, I didn't know that

[]: Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 3:12 pm BobBQ wrote: V wrote: ...why do you persistently state that the JSSDF if based on the military arm of the Nazi SS? serious question.

[]: Post by majlund » Wed Apr 30, 2008 4:15 pm It's actually quite simple. Elimination of noncombat personnel and on-the-spot execution of prisoners is very much like the Waffen-SS.

[]: Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 6:20 pm majlund wrote: It's actually quite simple. Elimination of noncombat personnel and on-the-spot execution of prisoners is very much like the Waffen-SS.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 30, 2008 7:13 pm TOPIC. We're not going any further on the JSSDF-Waffen SS angle in here.

[]: Post by RandomStuff » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:06 pm majlund wrote: It's actually quite simple. Elimination of noncombat personnel and on-the-spot execution of prisoners is very much like the Waffen-SS.

[]: Post by majlund » Thu May 01, 2008 4:55 am Almost every totalitarian regime has instituted an "elite" force with the following characteristics: - Superiority mentality. "Others" are viewed as less than human - Training program/missions ensuring brutality and ruthlessnes - Violent crimes to link the fate of the elite forces with the fate of the regime (Name of organisation withheld at moderators request) is just the most wellknown example, at least in the US and Europe. Note that it's quite possible to be brave and yet commit horrendous acts of inhumanity. (cynicism on) "Just following orders" is not a valid excuse - your only options is to win and thus control the way history is written and interpreted.(cynicism off) This is what makes the JSSDF in EoE so despicable. Sources I believe support my views: "Up Close and Personal" - David Lee "Das Reich" - Max Hastings "On Killing" - Dave Grossman "Saat in den Sturm" - Herbert Brunnegger "Nürnberginterviewene" - Leon Goldensohn (danish translation of "The Nuremberg Interviews")

[]: Post by Zeak » Thu May 01, 2008 11:06 pm I must say like the Eva JSSDF compaired to the real JSSDF. There Ice Brakers (Warships) look funny

[]: Post by Sachi » Thu May 01, 2008 11:07 pm Zeak wrote: I must say like the Eva JSSDF compaired to the real JSSDF. There Ice Brakers (Warships) look funny

[]: Post by Zeak » Thu May 01, 2008 11:10 pm Woops my bad but there the exact same thing anyway. Still it looks funny seing about 200 sailers on a bright orange boat.
